title: Equipment stat application, monster name variety, and attack speed visibility

requirements.text: Fix equipment stats not being applied to the character's effective stats. In lootGenerator.ts, add a base physical damage value to all generated Weapon items (scaled by rarity and item level). Add a base defense value to all generated Armor items. In combatEngine.ts, update the player stat derivation logic to iterate over all equipped items and sum their base values (physical damage for weapons, defense for armor) plus all affixes (Strength, Dexterity, Intelligence, Vitality, +HP, +Physical Damage, +Magic Damage, +Defense, +Critical Chance) on top of the character's base stats before any combat calculation. Ensure the character sheet display in CharacterSheet.tsx also reads these aggregated effective stats rather than base stats alone.

quotes: the equipment doesn't have physical damage dealt (weapons) and I'd guess the armor doesn't either
the stats the equipment has don't actually get added to your existing stats

acceptanceCriteria: Generated Weapon items have a non-zero base physical damage value visible in their tooltip.
Generated Armor items have a non-zero base defense value visible in their tooltip.
Equipping a weapon increases the player's effective physical damage in combat calculations.
Equipping armor increases the player's effective defense in combat calculations.
All affix stats (Str, Dex, Int, Vit, +HP, +PhysDmg, +MagDmg, +Def, +CritChance) on equipped items are added to effective stats.
CharacterSheet displays effective stats that include contributions from all equipped items.
Unequipping an item removes its stat contributions from effective stats.

requirements.text: Add a prefix/suffix name generation system for monsters in monsters.ts and wherever monster names are displayed in combatEngine.ts and DungeonRunScreen.tsx. Define a pool of at least 8 thematic prefixes (e.g. Vile, Rotting, Ancient, Cursed, Feral, Festering, Hungering, Corrupted) and at least 6 thematic suffixes (e.g. Brute, Warden, Scout, Shaman, Ravager, Specter). On each monster spawn, randomly combine a prefix and/or suffix with the base monster type name to produce a unique display name (e.g. 'Cursed Goblin Brute', 'Ancient Troll Shaman'). The base stat profile remains unchanged; only the displayed name varies.

quotes: there's a few more bug fixes as well like the lack of monster names. All I fight is a Goblin. Let's add some prefixes and suffixes for unique monster names.

acceptanceCriteria: No monster encounter displays only the bare base type name (e.g. 'Goblin' alone).
All 10 monster types receive prefix/suffix treatment.
Generated names feel thematically consistent with the dark fantasy setting.
Monster names with prefix/suffix appear correctly in the combat log and HP bar labels in DungeonRunScreen.
The prefix and suffix are selected randomly each time a monster is spawned.

requirements.text: Expose Ticks Between Attacks as a visible stat for both the player and each monster. In combatEngine.ts, ensure the player's attack interval (in ticks) is derivable from effective stats (e.g. based on Dexterity or a fixed base modified by stat scaling). On CharacterSheet.tsx, add a 'Ticks Between Attacks' row in the derived stats section showing the player's current value. In DungeonRunScreen.tsx, display each spawned monster's ticks-between-attacks value alongside its HP bar or in the combat info panel. Both values must be read directly from the same tick-rate logic used by the combat engine to stay accurate.

quotes: Can you also include the number of ticks between attacks as a viewable stat? I thought that was cool

acceptanceCriteria: CharacterSheet shows a 'Ticks Between Attacks' stat derived from the combat engine's actual tick-rate formula.
DungeonRunScreen shows each active monster's ticks-between-attacks alongside its HP bar or combat info.
The displayed value for both player and monsters matches the interval used during live combat simulation.
Changes to Dexterity (or whichever stat governs attack speed) are reflected in the displayed ticks value.

constraints: Do not alter the backend schema or Motoko actor for these changes — all three fixes are frontend-only.
Do not change the valid affix pool established in the previous patch (Strength, Dexterity, Intelligence, Vitality, +HP, +Physical Damage, +Magic Damage, +Defense, +Critical Chance).
Do not modify any files listed under frontend.immutablePaths.
nonGoals: Adding new monster types beyond the existing 10.
Changing monster base stat profiles or loot tables.
Backend changes to item or character storage.
Any new UI screens or navigation changes.
